channel.title: Polyglot Post
channel.description: Fresh takes for self-taught language learners.

item.title: Ask the forge: your grammar questions, answered
item.description: Straight answers to the grammar questions self-taught language learners keep asking, minus the hedging.

item.title: The quiet upgrade: small tweaks in immersion with outsized returns
item.description: No purchases, no overhauls — the modest immersion adjustments that self-taught language learners report mattering most.

item.title: Myth vs. reality: comprehensible input method
item.description: The persistent myths around comprehensible input method, stress-tested against what self-taught language learners actually report.

item.title: A weekend project: level up your methods in two hours
item.description: One contained, satisfying methods project for self-taught language learners — start Saturday morning, done by lunch.

item.title: Community pulse: what r/Anki is debating this week
item.description: The arguments, consensus shifts, and quietly useful threads from the grammar community this week.

item.title: The immersion checklist: run this before you spend
item.description: Seven questions that separate a justified immersion purchase from an expensive mood.

item.title: Five mistakes everyone makes in their first month of apps
item.description: The most common early stumbles in apps, and the cheap corrections that fix them for self-taught language learners.

item.title: "anki language setup" in practice: a real-world field test
item.description: We put the most-repeated advice about anki language setup through a month of actual use. Some of it survived.

item.title: Ask the forge: your grammar questions, answered
item.description: Straight answers to the grammar questions self-taught language learners keep asking, minus the hedging.

item.title: The quiet upgrade: small tweaks in immersion with outsized returns
item.description: No purchases, no overhauls — the modest immersion adjustments that self-taught language learners report mattering most.

item.title: Myth vs. reality: anki language setup
item.description: The persistent myths around anki language setup, stress-tested against what self-taught language learners actually report.

item.title: A weekend project: level up your methods in two hours
item.description: One contained, satisfying methods project for self-taught language learners — start Saturday morning, done by lunch.

item.title: Community pulse: what r/duolingo is debating this week
item.description: The arguments, consensus shifts, and quietly useful threads from the grammar community this week.

item.title: The immersion checklist: run this before you spend
item.description: Seven questions that separate a justified immersion purchase from an expensive mood.

item.title: Five mistakes everyone makes in their first month of apps
item.description: The most common early stumbles in apps, and the cheap corrections that fix them for self-taught language learners.

item.title: The state of Methods in 2026
item.description: A field report on methods: where things stand, and what self-taught language learners should do next.

item.title: Starter guide: Apps without the overwhelm
item.description: A practical on-ramp to apps for self-taught language learners — one repeatable workflow, no gear debt.

item.title: "comprehensible input method" — separating signal from noise
item.description: Thousands of people search "comprehensible input method" every month. Most of what they find is recycled. Here is what holds up.

item.title: Why self-taught language learners are rethinking Immersion
item.description: A quiet shift is underway in how self-taught language learners approach immersion — less maximalism, more maintenance.

item.title: Grammar on a budget: what actually matters
item.description: Where money changes outcomes in grammar — and the line items that are pure vanity.

item.title: What to watch next in Apps: five quiet shifts
item.description: Ignore the headlines — these are the slower currents reshaping apps for self-taught language learners.
